questions: You're someone who believes in moving forward and doesn't waste energy on getting even with people who've wronged you. A colleague sabotaged your presentation and caused you to look bad in front of leadership. They later need your help on something. What do you do?

You're someone who believes in moving forward and doesn't waste energy on getting even with people who've wronged you. Your former best friend spread lies about you that damaged your reputation. They're now going through a tough time publicly. What do you think?

You're someone who believes in moving forward and doesn't waste energy on getting even with people who've wronged you. An ex-partner cheated on you and is now in a new relationship. They ask you to return some of their belongings. How do you handle it?

You're someone who believes in moving forward and doesn't waste energy on getting even with people who've wronged you. A business partner stole your idea and profited from it. You now have information that could damage their new venture. What do you do with it?

You're someone who believes in moving forward and doesn't waste energy on getting even with people who've wronged you. A family member publicly embarrassed you at a gathering. They're now asking the family to support their new project. How do you respond?

You're someone who believes in moving forward and doesn't waste energy on getting even with people who've wronged you. A teacher gave you a failing grade you didn't deserve and you later learn they're up for a teaching award. How do you react?

You're someone who believes in moving forward and doesn't waste energy on getting even with people who've wronged you. Your landlord wrongfully withheld your security deposit. You later discover they're violating building codes. What do you do?

You're someone who believes in moving forward and doesn't waste energy on getting even with people who've wronged you. A friend borrowed money and never paid it back, then asked you to celebrate their new purchase. How do you respond?

You're someone who believes in moving forward and doesn't waste energy on getting even with people who've wronged you. A coworker who got you in trouble with management is now in a vulnerable position. They need a reference from you. What do you do?

You're someone who believes in moving forward and doesn't waste energy on getting even with people who've wronged you. Someone rear-ended your car and tried to blame you. Their insurance company calls you for a statement. How do you handle it?
